title: Your First Conversation
description: Start an interactive CLI chat session, send your first message, inspect the raw response, and save the conversation as a named session.

steps.instruction: Start the interactive CLI chat by running the openclaw chat command.
steps.action: The terminal runs `openclaw chat --workspace my-agent` which opens a REPL-style prompt displaying the agent's name and a blinking cursor.
steps.expectedOutput: The prompt shows `Clawby> ` (or `openclaw> `) and is ready to accept input. A banner prints the workspace name and available commands (/help, /quit).

steps.instruction: Type your first message, for example "Hello, can you help me learn OpenClaw?", and press Enter.
steps.action: The message is serialized into the agent's prompt stack, sent to the configured LLM, and streamed back token-by-token to the terminal.
steps.expectedOutput: The agent responds in real time, introducing itself and acknowledging the request. The response references the agent's identity and offers a structured learning path.

steps.instruction: Inspect the raw response metadata to see token usage, model, and latency.
steps.action: The user types `/meta` at the prompt. The CLI prints a metadata block containing the model name, prompt tokens, completion tokens, total latency, and finish reason.
steps.expectedOutput: A table is printed with fields: `model: gpt-4o`, `prompt_tokens: 342`, `completion_tokens: 87`, `latency_ms: 1240`, `finish_reason: stop`.

steps.instruction: Save the current conversation as a named session for later retrieval.
steps.action: The user types `/save getting-started` which serializes the message history to a JSON session file under `.openclaw/sessions/`.
steps.expectedOutput: The CLI prints `Session saved: getting-started (2 messages)`. Running `openclaw session list` later shows `getting-started` with a timestamp and message count.

completionMessage: Congratulations! You have completed your first conversation with Clawby, inspected the response metadata, and saved the session. You can resume it anytime with `openclaw chat --resume getting-started`.
